subject: Chemistry
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: Matter
topics: of matter, and each state is made up of fundamental units that define its characteristics.
activities: (solid, liquid, gas, plasma) and their characteristics.
Natural Materials: rock, Ice, metal, water, oil, and gases (air), wood
Artificial Materials: Graduated measuring cups/measuring cylinders/beakers, Balloons Thermometer, Bunsen burner or hot plate,
model or diagram (structure of atoms and molecules), plastic
assessment: A substance has a fixed shape and volume. What state of matter is it in?
Describe the differences in particle arrangement and motion between a solid, liquid, and gas.
A liquid is heated and turns into a gas. What is the process called, and what energy change occurs?
Explain why a solid maintains its shape, while a liquid takes the shape of its container.
Describe a real-world scenario where a substance changes from one state of matter to another.
